body:
“Oh my effing god! If you don’t stop leaving your hoodies at my apartment, I’m claiming them,” I tease her, tossing the well loved hoodie onto her head. Oh how it smells like her, vanilla, clove cigarettes, and her favorite coffee.

She pulls it off her head, making her short hair stick in odd directions. “You always say that,” she retorts, stretching out across my couch like it’s hers. And maybe it is now. Half our video collection is hers, and I think my cat of half a decade has somehow become more attached to her than me.

But honestly, I don’t really mind. There’s something about the fact she is so comfortable in my space, like it should be hers as well. It’s like how my fries are never just mine when she’s around, or the way she dances around my living room, whenever the right song starts playing.

I should let her know. I should say how my heart aches when she leans against me when we are curled up on the couch for a movie, how color of her eyes feel like they are engraved in my soul. But I stay silent. But I steal the hoodie again when she’s not looking. It will be nice to cuddle it when I go to bed tonight, just hoping s
reblog_comment:
“Oh my effing god! If you don’t stop leaving your hoodies at my apartment, I’m claiming them,” I tease her, tossing the well loved hoodie onto her head. Oh how it smells like her, vanilla, clove cigarettes, and her favorite coffee.

She pulls it off her head, making her short hair stick in odd directions. “You always say that,” she retorts, stretching out across my couch like it’s hers. And maybe it is now. Half our video collection is hers, and I think my cat of half a decade has somehow become more attached to her than me.

But honestly, I don’t really mind. There’s something about the fact she is so comfortable in my space, like it should be hers as well. It’s like how my fries are never just mine when she’s around, or the way she dances around my living room, whenever the right song starts playing.

I should let her know. I should say how my heart aches when she leans against me when we are curled up on the couch for a movie, how color of her eyes feel like they are engraved in my soul. But I stay silent. But I steal the hoodie again when she’s not looking. It will be nice to cuddle it when I go to bed tonight, just hoping scent of her as I slumber will have her in my dreams…
